behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: This failure depends on handoff across local components (file load + env overlay + validation + error formatting), so unit-only can miss path propagation bugs (MOCK-MISS). System was considered but rejected because the behavior text requires no real subprocess/network/external protocol contract; it only asserts local loader error content. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real config loader entry path with a temp config missing `database.host` and with related `TOOL_` env vars unset; assert the surfaced error includes the exact dotted path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is explicit cross-component config-path resolution using environment state plus loader path logic, so integration is the floor. System was considered but rejected: behavior text asks path selection, not external HTTP/subprocess contract or required external side-effect contract check. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by absence of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing `tool/config.yaml`, also place a different config under `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`; run loader and assert values come from the `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` location.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: This is direct precedence behavior across two sources (env and YAML), i.e., explicit cross-component handoff/state override, so unit-only is risky (MOCK-MISS). System was considered but rejected because no real external contract/OS-specific behavior is explicitly required beyond local composition. Agentic/Workflow do not apply.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a config file with a key (for example `database.host=file-host`) and set conflicting `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`; run loader and assert resulting effective config uses `env-host`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: To avoid false confidence, this should exercise the real YAML parser integration and error mapping, since a mocked parser exception can hide mismatch in line-number propagation (MOCK-HIDE). System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require subprocess/network/external response contract; it is local parsing/error surfacing. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create malformed YAML in a temp config file (known bad line), run loader, and assert the emitted error message includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Success is an externally visible OS filesystem side effect (directory/file creation) that must be proven by round-trip observation, which triggers the System triad (REAL-INFRA + ROUND-TRIP EFFECT RULE). Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly requires visible creation/write-before-proceeding semantics at startup, not just in-process logic.
behaviors.test_description: In an isolated real filesystem environment where config dir is absent, run the actual CLI startup path; assert the directory is created, defaults file exists with expected structure/content, and startup proceeds afterward (e.g., command continues rather than exiting).
